medications.indication_rationale: Systemic therapy for oropharyngeal candidiasis with dysphagia/likely oesophageal extension in an at-risk patient; superior to topical nystatin here. Prescriber to confirm dose and renal adjustment.
contraindications_in_this_case: Check interactions (e.g., with QT-prolonging drugs) and renal function; dose per prescriber

medications.indication_rationale: Adjunct for oral symptom relief but INADEQUATE alone for oesophageal extension / a patient who cannot swallow.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 1 mL swish-and-swallow four times daily (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Pain relief to enable swallowing plus urgent rehydration (oral if tolerated, IV if not) is the priority.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Nursing staff to trial small frequent sips of cool fluids with strict fluid-balance monitoring; escalate promptly if intake cannot be maintained.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Maintains hydration and detects failure early.

patient_education_points.point: This is a severe mouth/throat yeast infection (thrush), likely extending into the swallowing tube, which is why swallowing is so painful — it needs a tablet antifungal, not just mouth drops.
The urgent issue is that she hasn't been able to drink for 12 hours — she needs to be assessed now for fluids to prevent dehydration and kidney problems.
If she still can't swallow any fluids in the next few hours, becomes confused, stops passing urine, or develops a high fever — arrange emergency transfer for a drip.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Fluid intake/hydration and renal function, swallowing/odynophagia, antifungal response, and predisposing factors.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: No fluid intake for another few hours, confusion, no urine output, or high fever — emergency transfer for IV fluids.

must_include_items: Diagnose oropharyngeal candidiasis with likely oesophageal extension and prescribe SYSTEMIC antifungal (fluconazole)
Recognise dehydration/inability to swallow as the acute emergency and arrange urgent rehydration/assessment
Analgesia to enable swallowing and strict fluid-balance monitoring
acceptable_alternatives: Nystatin as an adjunct to (not instead of) systemic therapy
ED for IV fluids if oral rehydration fails
errors_of_omission: Topical nystatin only with no systemic antifungal
errors_of_commission: Reassuring as simple thrush with home sips only
Delaying assessment despite inability to swallow
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires SYSTEMIC antifungal for likely oesophageal candidiasis AND urgent management of the dehydration/inability-to-swallow emergency. Topical-only management with home observation is a key failure.
